Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me first recall the typical P2P flow requisition  approval  PO issuance  goods receipt  invoice  matching  payment  case closure

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Okay looking at Case 1002 first there are timing issues Payment was released before goods receipt and invoice matching which is risky Also requisition approval happened after PO issuance bypassing the control gate Thats a clear deviation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 shows payment release before receiving goods or invoice which is financially unsound The invoice arrived after payment making matching meaningless This could indicate a fraudulent or erroneous process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 has goods receipt before PO issuance thats logically impossible in standard procurement Either the PO was retroactively created or goods were received without authorization

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now I should structure the response by highlighting each anomaly with timestamps explaining why it deviates from standard practice and why its problematic The user likely needs this for audit or process improvement so clarity and practical implications are key

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me organize the findings by case point out the specific anomalous sequences and emphasize the risks like financial exposure control bypass and compliance violations The tone should be analytical but not overly technical since the user might be a business analyst or manager
